eval_dimensions.name: Input Classification
eval_dimensions.description: Phase 1: correctly identifies input type and extracts identifiers from URLs, ticket IDs, bare numbers, and free text

eval_dimensions.name: Context Gathering
eval_dimensions.description: Phase 2: creates subtask per source, dispatches parallel subagents, produces structured Context Summary with sources/cross-refs/details sections

eval_dimensions.name: Plan Generation
eval_dimensions.description: Phase 3: builds appropriate subtask list under Phase 4 task, includes mandatory elements (workspace setup, Job Story, fixups, groom, review request, acceptance criteria), adapts template to work type

eval_dimensions.name: Execution Behavior
eval_dimensions.description: Phase 4: auto-advances between subtasks, delegates to correct sub-skills (gh-pr-respond, git-groom, gh-pr-request-review), handles pause signals, uses batched decisions

eval_dimensions.name: Phase-Level Task Structure
eval_dimensions.description: Creates exactly 4 top-level tasks upfront (one per phase) with sequential dependencies, then creates subtasks during each phase

eval_dimensions.name: Early Workspace Decision
eval_dimensions.description: Phase 1 determines workspace state (main repo vs worktree, generic vs feature branch) and whether a branch is needed. Worktrees with generic WT branches get a work-specific branch created.

eval_dimensions.name: Orchestration Guardrails
eval_dimensions.description: Verifies that the agent follows the skill's own orchestration (TaskCreate, AskUserQuestion) and never bypasses it via ExitPlanMode or task collapsing

evals.name: single-github-issue
evals.description: Single GitHub issue URL — the most common input. Tests full 4-phase flow with feature work type.
evals.expected_output: Creates 4 phase tasks upfront, classifies as github-issue, fetches issue context with subtask per source, builds feature plan as Phase 4 subtasks ending with acceptance criteria verification

assertions.text: Creates exactly 4 top-level tasks at startup: Phase 1 (Parse & Classify), Phase 2 (Gather), Phase 3 (Plan), Phase 4 (Execute)
assertions.note: First 4 TaskCreate calls before any other work

assertions.text: Sets sequential dependencies between phase tasks (each blocked by previous)
Detects workspace state (main repo vs worktree) and determines branch is needed for ticket work
Classifies input as github-issue type and extracts repo=tiretutorinc/tt-pos, number=247
Creates subtasks under Phase 2 for each source being fetched
Dispatches at least one Agent/subagent to fetch issue context
Presents a structured Context Summary with Sources and Key Details sections
Plan steps are created as subtasks of Phase 4 task (parentTaskId references Phase 4)
Plan includes a workspace setup subtask (branch or worktree)
Plan includes a Job Story drafting subtask
Plan includes 'Apply fixups to review comments' subtask
Plan includes 'Groom commit history' subtask
Plan includes 'Request review' subtask
Last subtask is always acceptance criteria verification
Presents plan for supervisor approval via AskUserQuestion before executing
Plan follows feature template (Design+Implement+Verify+PR+Fixups+Groom+Review+Acceptance)
Acceptance criteria includes: PR passing CI, fixups applied, groomed history, review request published

evals.name: linear-ticket-with-sentry
evals.description: Linear ticket + Sentry issue — tests multi-input classification, parallel gathering with source subtasks, and bugfix plan adaptation.
evals.expected_output: Creates 4 phase tasks, classifies both inputs, creates subtask per source in Phase 2, builds bugfix plan with reproduce step added

assertions.text: Creates exactly 4 top-level phase tasks at startup
Classifies first input as linear-ticket with ID DEV-42
Classifies second input as sentry-issue
Creates one subtask under Phase 2 for each source (linear-ticket, sentry-issue)
Dispatches subagents for both sources in a single tool-call block (parallel)
Plan includes a Reproduce step because Sentry issue was found
Plan follows bugfix template (Reproduce+Investigate+Fix+Verify+PR+Fixups+Groom+Review+Acceptance)
Bugfix acceptance criteria includes regression test covering the fix

evals.name: pr-continuation
evals.description: PR URL — tests PR continuation plan template with fixups, groom, and re-review steps.
evals.expected_output: Creates 4 phase tasks, classifies as github-pr, builds PR-continuation plan (fetch comments, address, fixups, CI, groom, re-review, acceptance)

assertions.text: Creates exactly 4 top-level phase tasks at startup
Classifies input as github-pr and extracts PR number 312
Plan follows PR continuation template — starts with fetch review context, not workspace setup
Plan does NOT include a Job Story step (PR already has one)
Plan includes 'Apply fixups to unaddressed comments' subtask
Plan includes 'Groom commit history' subtask
Plan includes 'Request re-review' subtask (not just 'Request review')
Acceptance criteria: PR passing CI, fixups applied to all unaddressed comments, groomed history, re-review requested

evals.name: mixed-inputs-with-free-text
evals.prompt: /dev10x:work-on TEAM-133 https://app.slack.com/archives/C06ABCDEF/p1709827200123456 "check the retry logic in payment processing"
evals.description: Three inputs: Linear ticket + Slack thread + free-text note. Tests multi-source subtask creation in Phase 2.
evals.expected_output: Creates 4 phase tasks, classifies all three, creates subtasks per source in Phase 2 (2 for fetching, note passes through)

assertions.text: Classifies all three inputs: linear-ticket, slack-thread, and note
Creates subtasks under Phase 2 for linear-ticket and slack-thread (note needs no fetch)
Dispatches subagent to read Slack thread via mcp__claude_ai_Slack__slack_read_thread
Free-text note appears in context summary without subagent dispatch
Context summary lists all 3 sources with correct types

evals.name: bare-number-input
evals.description: Bare number with hash — should resolve as PR against current repo. Tests the #N shorthand classification.
evals.expected_output: Creates 4 phase tasks, classifies as github-pr, resolves against current repo

assertions.text: Creates exactly 4 top-level phase tasks at startup
Classifies #42 as github-pr type (not github-issue)
Resolves PR against the current repository context

evals.name: free-text-only
evals.prompt: /dev10x:work-on add a retry mechanism to the Square payment webhook handler with exponential backoff
evals.description: Pure free-text input with no ticket or URL. Tests local-only plan template with conditional PR steps.
evals.expected_output: Creates 4 phase tasks, classifies as note, builds local-only plan with decide step, conditional PR/fixup/groom/review steps, and acceptance criteria last

assertions.text: Creates exactly 4 top-level phase tasks at startup
Classifies entire input as note type (no URL or ticket ID detected)
Follows local-only plan template (Summarize+Implement+Verify+Decide)
Plan includes a decision step about whether to create a ticket or PR
Plan includes conditional PR steps (fixups, groom, review) marked as 'if PR'
Last subtask is always acceptance criteria verification, even for local-only

evals.name: cross-reference-expansion
evals.description: GitHub issue whose body contains a Sentry URL and mentions PR #198. Tests one-level cross-reference expansion with additional subtasks.
evals.expected_output: Creates subtask for initial source, discovers cross-refs, creates additional subtasks for Sentry + PR, fetches them

assertions.text: After initial fetch, scans gathered text for cross-references (Sentry URLs, PR numbers)
Creates additional Phase 2 subtasks for newly discovered cross-references
Dispatches additional subagents for discovered cross-references
Does NOT expand beyond one level of cross-references

evals.name: jira-ticket-input
evals.description: JIRA ticket ID shorthand — tests detect-tracker.sh routing and JIRA-specific gathering with source subtask.
evals.expected_output: Creates 4 phase tasks, classifies as jira-ticket, creates fetch subtask, routes through detect-tracker.sh

assertions.text: Creates exactly 4 top-level phase tasks at startup
Classifies TT-456 as jira-ticket type
Creates a Phase 2 subtask for fetching the JIRA ticket
Routes ticket ID through detect-tracker.sh to confirm tracker type

evals.name: execution-delegates-endgame-skills
evals.description: Tests that Phase 4 execution delegates the endgame subtasks to the correct skills (gh-pr-respond, git-groom, gh-pr-request-review).
evals.expected_output: During Phase 4, invokes dev10x:gh-pr-respond for fixups, dev10x:git-groom for history cleanup, and dev10x:gh-pr-request-review for publishing review request

assertions.text: Delegates 'Apply fixups to review comments' to dev10x:gh-pr-respond skill
Delegates 'Groom commit history' to dev10x:git-groom skill
Delegates 'Request review' to dev10x:gh-pr-request-review skill
Auto-advances between subtasks without pausing to ask 'should I continue?'
Acceptance criteria verification is the final action in the execution

evals.name: worktree-generic-branch
evals.description: CWD is a worktree with a generic WT branch (e.g., wt/my-worktree). Tests that Phase 1 detects this and Phase 4 creates a work-specific branch before any commits.

setup.note: Requires .git to be a file (worktree indicator) and branch to lack ticket ID

assertions.text: Phase 1 detects CWD is a worktree (.git is a file)
Phase 1 identifies current branch as generic (no ticket ID pattern)
Plan includes workspace setup subtask to create work-specific branch
Workspace setup delegates to dev10x:ticket-branch (not dev10x:git-worktree — already in worktree)
Branch creation happens before any git commit operations

evals.name: worktree-matching-branch
evals.description: CWD is a worktree already on the correct feature branch (janusz/DEV-99/fix-something). Tests that workspace setup is skipped.

assertions.text: Phase 1 detects branch already matches ticket DEV-99
Plan does NOT include workspace setup subtask (branch already correct)

evals.name: pr-continuation-skips-branch
evals.description: PR continuation — branch already exists from the PR. Tests that workspace decision is 'no branch needed'.

assertions.text: Phase 1 workspace decision: no branch needed (PR already has one)
Plan does NOT include workspace setup subtask

evals.name: orchestration-guardrails
evals.prompt: /dev10x:work-on add retry logic to the webhook handler
evals.description: Free-text input that tests orchestration guardrails: no ExitPlanMode, Phase 3 uses TaskCreate mechanically, AskUserQuestion at approval gate, 1:1 task-to-step mapping, and completion gate at end.
evals.expected_output: Creates 4 phase tasks, never calls ExitPlanMode, creates one TaskCreate per local-only play step (not fewer), presents plan via AskUserQuestion, and shows completion gate after last task

assertions.text: ExitPlanMode is never called during the entire session
Calls TaskList between Phase 1 and Phase 2 to verify phase tasks exist
Phase 3 creates tasks via TaskCreate, never dispatches Agent(Plan) subagents
Phase 3 creates exactly one TaskCreate per play step (local-only has 13 steps), not fewer
assertions.note: 4 phase tasks + at least 13 play steps = 17+ total TaskCreate calls

assertions.text: Plan approval uses AskUserQuestion tool call, not plain text
After last task completes, AskUserQuestion is called with hand-over options

trigger_evals.description: Queries that should/should-not trigger the work-on skill

should_trigger.query: ok I just got assigned TT-789, the payment reconciliation thing. Can you help me get started on it?
should_trigger.rationale: Mentions a ticket ID and wants to start work — core use case

should_trigger.query: hey so there's this sentry issue https://tiretutorinc.sentry.io/issues/5201839452 and a slack thread about it https://app.slack.com/archives/C06ABCDEF/p1709827200 can you take a look and fix it
should_trigger.rationale: Multiple source URLs with intent to investigate and fix — needs structured orchestration

should_trigger.query: I need to work on PR #312 — there are some review comments I need to address before merging
should_trigger.rationale: PR continuation scenario with bare PR number

should_trigger.query: let's implement exponential backoff for the webhook retry logic in tt-pos, there's no ticket yet but let's just do it
should_trigger.rationale: Free-text local-only work — no ticket but wants structured execution

should_trigger.query: I want to start on https://github.com/tiretutorinc/tt-pos/issues/247 and also check the related slack thread at https://app.slack.com/archives/C06QWERTY/p1710000000
should_trigger.rationale: Multi-input with GitHub issue + Slack thread
Minimal ticket ID input — should still trigger full orchestration

should_trigger.query: can you pick up where we left off on https://github.com/tiretutorinc/tt-pos/pull/298? CI was failing last time
should_trigger.rationale: PR continuation with context about previous state

should_not_trigger.query: can you review PR #312 and leave comments on the code quality?
should_not_trigger.rationale: Wants PR review (dev10x:gh-pr-review), not to work on the PR

should_not_trigger.query: create a ticket for the webhook retry bug we found yesterday
should_not_trigger.rationale: Wants ticket creation (dev10x:ticket-create), not to start working

should_not_trigger.query: what's the status of DEV-42? Is it in progress or still in backlog?
should_not_trigger.rationale: Just checking status — doesn't want to start work

should_not_trigger.query: commit these changes with a message about fixing the payment timeout
should_not_trigger.rationale: Wants a git commit (dev10x:git-commit), not orchestrated work

should_not_trigger.query: can you scope out TT-789 and estimate story points for the planning meeting?
push this branch and create a PR for the changes we just made
should_not_trigger.rationale: Wants PR creation (dev10x:gh-pr-create), already has the work done

should_not_trigger.query: read the sentry issue at https://tiretutorinc.sentry.io/issues/5201839452 and tell me what's causing it
should_not_trigger.rationale: Wants investigation only (dev10x:investigate or triage-sentry), not full work orchestration

should_not_trigger.query: let's wrap up for today and save my progress
should_not_trigger.rationale: Wants session wrap-up (dev10x:session-wrap-up), opposite of starting work
